_note: All hardware-dependent values (UART ports, GPIO pins) are null until wired. The code will error clearly at runtime if a null value is used in mission mode.
camera_type_note: Which camera is wired: 'a' = OV2311 USB (default, cv2.VideoCapture) | 'b' = IMX708 CSI (picamera2). Camera A needs camera_device set below. Camera B needs python3-picamera2 installed and dtoverlay=imx708 in /boot/firmware/config.txt.
camera_device_note: USB camera device index. Run: ls /dev/video* before plugging in the camera, then again after — the new entry that appears is your device (e.g. /dev/video0 → index 0, /dev/video2 → index 2). Default 0 is correct if no other USB video devices are connected.
resolution_note: Camera capture resolution [width, height]. [1600, 1200] is the OV2311 native max and the default. Lower to [1280, 720] if the Pi is CPU-bottlenecked at full resolution. If you change this, update frame_width_px and frame_height_px in the mission block to match.
framerate_note: Camera frames per second. 50 is the OV2311 native rate. Reduce to 30 on a heavily loaded Pi to free CPU between frames.
board_roi_note: Optional [x, y, w, h] crop rectangle. Limits Tesseract to the board area only — the single biggest OCR speedup if the board occupies a known region of the frame. null = full frame.
ocr_frame_interval_note: Run Tesseract every N frames. Frames between runs return the cached result instantly. Raise to 8–10 to reduce CPU load; lower to 3 for more responsive detection. At 30 fps, 5 = ~6 OCR runs/s.
ocr_scale_note: Scale factor applied before OCR preprocessing. 0.5 = 800x600 from 1600x1200. Try 0.25 (400x300) if OCR is still slow — whiteboard marker text is large enough at quarter resolution. Do not go below 0.25 or word detection degrades.
yolo_weights_note: Path to the exported NCNN model directory (contains *.param and *.bin). Train via train/train_colab.ipynb, export to NCNN format, then copy the output folder here.
yolo_conf_note: Detection confidence threshold (0.0–1.0). Detections below this score are ignored. Override at runtime with --conf flag. Lower to 0.35 if the board is being missed; raise to 0.55 to reduce false positives.
display_note: Show live OpenCV window during inference and mission. Set to false on a headless Pi (no monitor connected) — OpenCV will crash if true when no display is available.
output_dir_note: Directory for annotated frames saved with --save flag (created automatically). Not used in mission mode.

range_sensor._note: Forward range sensor used by the mission approach controller and sensor_ocr_test.py. Set type to match your hardware. Sensor A (UART) fields: uart, baud. Sensor B (I2C) fields: i2c_address, timing_budget_ms.
range_sensor.type_note: Which range sensor is wired: 'a' = TF-Luna / TFMini (UART, default)  |  'b' = VL53L3CX (I2C).
range_sensor.uart_note: Sensor A — required. UART device path for the TF sensor. Run ls -l /dev/ttyAMA* to list all UARTs. The number matches your dtoverlay line — e.g. dtoverlay=uart3 gives /dev/ttyAMA3.
range_sensor.baud_note: Sensor A. Baud rate for the TF-Luna / TFMini. Fixed at 115200 by hardware — do not change.
range_sensor.distance_threshold_m_note: Sensor A. Distance at which cleaning triggers in preflight_test.py. Set to match approach_stop_dist_m so the preflight test mirrors real mission behaviour.
range_sensor.i2c_address_note: Sensor B only. Default I2C address is 0x29 (decimal 41). Only change if the XSHUT pin was used to remap the address. Wiring: VIN→3.3V (pin 1), GND→GND (pin 6), SDA→GPIO2 (pin 3), SCL→GPIO3 (pin 5).
range_sensor.timing_budget_ms_note: Sensor B only. Measurement timing budget in milliseconds — controls speed vs accuracy tradeoff. Lower = faster readings, less accurate; higher = slower, more accurate. Valid range: 10–200 ms. 50 ms gives ~20 readings/s, good for indoor approach. Raise to 100 ms if range readings are unstable.

wiper._note: Wiper relay settings (wiper.py). GPIO on/off relay: turns on for wipe_duration_s then off. Set wiper_gpio_pin once physically connected.
wiper.wiper_gpio_pin_note: BCM GPIO pin number wired to the wiper relay IN terminal. Example: 17. Assign once physically connected.
wiper.wipe_duration_s_note: How long to run the wiper relay per cleaning cycle (seconds). Wiper turns on for this duration then off.

mission._note: Autonomous drone mission settings (python main.py --mode mission). All null values must be filled in before running. Run 'ls -l /dev/ttyAMA*' on the Pi to list all UARTs. Use /dev/ttyAMAX paths — the number matches your dtoverlay=uartX line in config.txt.
mission.mavlink_uart_note: UART port connecting the Pi to the ArduPilot flight controller. Run ls -l /dev/ttyAMA* to find the path — e.g. /dev/ttyAMA0. Must match the FC TELEM port — see README Setup Step 3.
mission.mavlink_baud_note: Baud rate for the ArduPilot MAVLink connection. Must match SERIALx_BAUD on the FC. 57600 is the ArduPilot default for companion computers.
mission.takeoff_altitude_m_note: Target hover altitude in metres after arming. 1.5 m gives clearance for indoor scanning without hitting the ceiling.
mission.scan_yaw_rate_dps_note: Yaw spin rate in degrees/second during SCAN. 20 dps = one full rotation in 18 s. Slower means more time per frame and less chance of missing the board.
mission.scan_timeout_s_note: Abort SCAN and RTL if no dirty board is found within this many seconds.
mission.approach_stop_dist_m_note: Stop forward approach when the range sensor reads this distance to the board (metres). Set to the reach of the wiper arm.
mission.approach_kp_note: Proportional gain for lateral and vertical centering during APPROACH. Tune down if the drone oscillates sideways.
mission.approach_kp_forward_note: Proportional gain for forward speed: vx = kp_forward × (dist − stop_dist). Naturally decelerates to zero at the board. Tune down if approach feels jerky.
mission.align_threshold_px_note: Phase 1 tolerance: drone will not move forward until the board centre is within this many pixels of frame centre on both axes.
mission.approach_max_speed_ms_note: Hard velocity cap on any axis during approach (m/s). The P controller never exceeds this regardless of distance.
mission.approach_cautious_speed_ms_note: Forward creep speed used when the range sensor has not yet returned a valid reading. Intentionally very slow.
mission.pump_gpio_pin_note: BCM GPIO pin wired to the pump relay IN terminal. Example: 17. Assign once the relay is physically connected.
mission.pump_duration_s_note: How long to run the pump during CLEAN (seconds). Runs before the wiper sweep.
mission.detection_model_note: Which model to use for dirty board detection: 'yolo' = YOLO NCNN (default, faster, visual) | 'ocr' = Tesseract OCR (text-based). Both detect dirty boards and transition through the same states.
mission.clean_timeout_s_note: Maximum time in CLEAN state (seconds). Forces RETURN if cleaning has not completed within this window — prevents looping indefinitely on a board that cannot be fully cleaned.
mission.cleaning_cooldown_s_note: Preflight test only. Minimum seconds between cleaning cycles. Prevents re-triggering on every dirty frame.
mission.frame_width_px_note: Frame width used by the approach P controller to compute pixel error from centre. Must match resolution[0] above.
mission.frame_height_px_note: Frame height used by the approach P controller. Must match resolution[1] above.
mission.post_clean_retreat_m_note: Distance to fly backwards in body frame after a clean cycle (metres). 0.3 m ≈ 1 foot. Drone does not yaw — moves straight back from cleaning position.
mission.post_clean_retreat_speed_ms_note: Speed for the post-clean backward retreat (m/s). 0.3 m/s is slow and controlled — gives ~1 second of movement for the default 0.3 m distance.
